description: Deep-dive research into TurboQuant (KV cache) and RaBitQ (ANN search) quantization algorithms, with C++/CUDA implementation for GLM5 and DeepSeekV3.2

researchGuidelines.targetAudience: Someone with Calculus 3 and Linear Algebra background who may need refreshers on specific concepts
researchGuidelines.mathFormatting: All math must be formatted in LaTeX within Markdown (inline: $...$ , display: $$...$$)
researchGuidelines.comprehensiveness: Thorough coverage without verbosity. Every claim should be justified. Include refreshers on prerequisite concepts when needed.
researchGuidelines.sources: Cite all web sources with URLs. For local code analysis, reference specific file paths.
researchGuidelines.outputFormat: Markdown files in research/ directory with proper LaTeX math rendering

userStories.title: Research Shannon's Source Coding Theory
userStories.description: Understand Shannon's foundational work on lossy compression and distortion-rate functions. Write for someone with Calc 3 + Linear Algebra who needs concept refreshers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/01-shannon-source-coding.md with LaTeX-formatted math
Include refresher: What is entropy? What is information content?
Explain distortion-rate function D(R) with intuitive buildup
Document Shannon Lower Bound (Lemma 2 from paper) with step-by-step derivation
Include worked numerical example showing the bound in action
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research Beta Distribution on Hyperspheres
userStories.description: Understand why random rotation induces Beta distribution on coordinates. Build intuition from Linear Algebra foundations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/02-hypersphere-distributions.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What is an orthogonal matrix? Why does Qx preserve ||x||?
Refresher: What is a hypersphere S^(d-1)? Visualize for d=2,3
Explain Gamma function Γ(z) with examples: Γ(1)=1, Γ(1/2)=√π, Γ(n)=(n-1)!
Show convergence to N(0, 1/d) for large d with numerical examples
Explain concentration of measure intuitively
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research Lloyd-Max Scalar Quantization
userStories.description: Understand optimal scalar quantization and codebook construction as a continuous k-means problem.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/03-lloyd-max-quantization.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What is k-means clustering? What is the objective?
Explain quantization as partitioning the real line into intervals
Derive the k-means formulation (Equation 4 from paper)
Explain Voronoi tessellation: why boundaries are midpoints between centroids
Show the Lloyd-Max iterative algorithm step-by-step
Compute optimal codebooks for b=1,2,3,4 bits (include actual numerical values)
Explain Panter-Dite high-resolution formula with derivation sketch
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research QJL Transform
userStories.description: Understand Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss for 1-bit inner product quantization.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/04-qjl-transform.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What is an inner product ⟨x,y⟩? Geometric interpretation
Refresher: What is the Johnson-Lindenstrauss lemma? Why does random projection preserve distances?
Document Definition 1: Qqjl(x) = sign(S·x) where S has i.i.d. N(0,1) entries
Prove unbiasedness: E[⟨y, Q^(-1)(Q(x))⟩] = ⟨y,x⟩ step-by-step
Derive variance bound: Var ≤ (π/2d)·||y||²
Explain why sign() preserves inner product information (intuition + math)
Include reference to QJL paper (arXiv:2406.03482)
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research MSE vs Inner Product Bias Problem
userStories.description: Understand why MSE-optimal quantizers are biased for inner products and how the two-stage approach fixes this.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/05-mse-vs-inner-product-bias.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What does 'unbiased estimator' mean? E[θ̂] = θ
Work through the 1-bit example completely: show E[⟨y, Q^(-1)(Q(x))⟩] = (2/π)·⟨y,x⟩
Explain where the 2/π factor comes from geometrically
Show how bias decreases with higher bit-widths (table of bias factors)
Explain the two-stage solution: MSE(b-1) + QJL(1) on residual
Prove why this combination is unbiased
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Document TurboQuant_mse Algorithm
userStories.description: Fully document Algorithm 1 (MSE-optimized TurboQuant) with annotated pseudocode.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/06-turboquant-mse-algorithm.md with LaTeX math
Annotated pseudocode for Algorithm 1 with explanation of each line
Explain rotation matrix generation via QR decomposition
Refresher: What is QR decomposition? Why does it give orthogonal Q?
Document Quant_mse procedure: rotate → find nearest centroids → store indices
Document DeQuant_mse procedure: lookup centroids → rotate back
Walk through Theorem 1 proof (distortion bound) step-by-step
Include worked example: quantize a specific vector x with b=2 bits
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Document TurboQuant_prod Algorithm
userStories.description: Fully document Algorithm 2 (inner-product-optimized TurboQuant) with annotated pseudocode.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/07-turboquant-prod-algorithm.md with LaTeX math
Annotated pseudocode for Algorithm 2 with explanation of each line
Explain the two-stage structure: why use (b-1) bits for MSE and 1 bit for QJL
Document residual computation: r = x - Q^(-1)_mse(Q_mse(x))
Explain what is stored: (idx, qjl, ||r||) and why we need the norm
Document Quant_prod and DeQuant_prod procedures
Walk through Theorem 2 proof (unbiasedness + distortion bound)
Include worked example: quantize a vector and verify unbiasedness numerically
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Document Lower Bounds and Optimality
userStories.description: Understand information-theoretic lower bounds and verify TurboQuant's near-optimality.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/08-lower-bounds-optimality.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What is Yao's minimax principle? Relate randomized algorithms to distributions
Document Theorem 3 (lower bounds) with full proof
Show the connection to Shannon Lower Bound from US-001
Calculate the approximation factor: TurboQuant achieves ≈2.7× optimal
Create table comparing TurboQuant distortion to lower bound for b=1,2,3,4,5
Explain why the gap is smaller at lower bit-widths
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research RaBitQ Core Theory: Normalization and Codebook
userStories.description: Understand RaBitQ's foundational approach: normalizing vectors to unit hypersphere and constructing bi-valued codebook with random rotation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/09-rabitq-normalization-codebook.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: Why normalize vectors? Centroid-based normalization
Derive the distance reduction formula: ||o_r - q_r||² = ||o_r - c||² + ||q_r - c||² - 2·||o_r - c||·||q_r - c||·⟨o,q⟩
Explain the bi-valued codebook C = {+1/√D, -1/√D}^D
Why does C have exactly 2^D vectors? Why are they unit vectors?
Explain random orthogonal rotation P and C_rand = {Px | x ∈ C}
Connection to Johnson-Lindenstrauss transformation
Why rotation removes preference for specific vectors (geometric intuition)
Include worked example: normalize a 4D vector, show codebook structure
Cite RaBitQ paper (arXiv:2405.12497) and all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research RaBitQ Quantization: Finding Nearest Codebook Vector
userStories.description: Understand how RaBitQ efficiently finds the nearest codebook vector and stores it as a D-bit string.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/10-rabitq-quantization.md with LaTeX math
Derive: x̄ = argmax_{x∈C} ⟨o, Px⟩ = argmax_{x∈C} ⟨P⁻¹o, x⟩
Explain why we inverse-transform the data vector instead of transforming the codebook
Show that optimal x̄ has signs matching signs of P⁻¹o
Explain the D-bit string representation x̄_b ∈ {0,1}^D
Relationship: x̄ = (2x̄_b - 1_D)/√D
Document what is stored: quantization code x̄_b, precomputed ||o_r - c|| and ⟨ō,o⟩
Include worked example: quantize a specific unit vector step-by-step
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research RaBitQ Unbiased Estimator and Error Bound
userStories.description: Understand RaBitQ's unbiased inner product estimator and its sharp theoretical error bound.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/11-rabitq-estimator-error-bound.md with LaTeX math
Derive the geometric relationship (Lemma 3.1): ⟨ō,q⟩ = ⟨ō,o⟩·⟨o,q⟩ + ⟨ō,e₁⟩·√(1-⟨o,q⟩²)
Explain e₁ = (q - ⟨q,o⟩o)/||q - ⟨q,o⟩o|| geometrically
Prove unbiasedness: E[⟨ō,q⟩/⟨ō,o⟩] = ⟨o,q⟩ (Theorem 3.2)
Why E[⟨ō,o⟩] ≈ 0.8 and E[⟨ō,e₁⟩] = 0?
Document the error bound: |⟨ō,q⟩/⟨ō,o⟩ - ⟨o,q⟩| = O(1/√D) with high probability
Explain why this is asymptotically optimal (matches lower bound)
Contrast with PQ: why simply using ⟨ō,q⟩ ≈ ⟨o,q⟩ is BIASED
Include numerical verification: sample P many times, plot ⟨ō,o⟩ and ⟨ō,e₁⟩ distributions
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research RaBitQ Query Quantization and Efficient Computation
userStories.description: Understand how RaBitQ quantizes query vectors and computes distances efficiently using bitwise operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/12-rabitq-query-computation.md with LaTeX math
Explain query transformation: q' = P⁻¹q
Document uniform scalar quantization of q' to B_q-bit unsigned integers
Explain randomized rounding: q̄_u[i] = floor((q'[i] - v_l)/Δ + u_i)
Prove Theorem 3.3: B_q = Θ(log log D) suffices for negligible error
Why B_q = 4 is sufficient in practice
Derive the efficient computation formula:
Explain bitwise decomposition: ⟨x̄_b, q̄_u⟩ = Σ_{j=0}^{B_q-1} 2^j · ⟨x̄_b, q̄_u^(j)⟩
Show how inner product of binary vectors → bitwise AND + popcount
Include worked example: compute ⟨ō,q⟩ for specific vectors
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research RaBitQ SIMD FastScan Implementation
userStories.description: Understand RaBitQ's SIMD-based batch distance computation using FastScan technique.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/13-rabitq-fastscan-simd.md with LaTeX math
Explain why batch processing enables SIMD optimization
Document the LUT (Look-Up Table) approach: split D-bit string into D/4 sub-segments
Each LUT has 2^4 = 16 entries for inner products with 4-bit patterns
Explain packing 32 quantization codes in a batch
How AVX2 256-bit registers host two LUTs
Document shuffle instruction for parallel lookup
Compare efficiency: bitwise (single vector) vs SIMD (batch)
Reference PQx4fs implementation strategy
Include diagram: LUT structure and SIMD register layout
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research RaBitQ with IVF Index Integration
userStories.description: Understand how RaBitQ integrates with Inverted File (IVF) index for ANN search.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/14-rabitq-ivf-integration.md with LaTeX math
Explain IVF structure: cluster data vectors with KMeans, build bucket per cluster
Document per-cluster normalization: use cluster centroid for normalization
Explain query phase: select N_probe nearest clusters, search within each
Document error-bound-based re-ranking (unique to RaBitQ)
Why PQ needs empirical re-rank threshold but RaBitQ uses theoretical bounds
Compare: IVF-PQ vs IVF-RaBitQ workflow
Document the complete search algorithm (Algorithm 2 from paper)
Explain parameters: N_probe, k (top-k), ε₀ (confidence)
Include performance comparison table from paper
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Analyze RaBitQ Reference Implementation (search.cpp)
userStories.description: Study the RaBitQ reference C++ implementation to understand practical details.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/15-rabitq-implementation-analysis.md
Fetch and analyze https://github.com/gaoj0017/RaBitQ/blob/main/src/search.cpp
Document the IVFRN class structure and key methods
Explain the search() function workflow
Document getRatio() and result heap usage
Analyze the rotation: RandQ = mul(RandQ, P)
Document precomputed values: transformation matrix P, ||o_r - c||, ⟨ō,o⟩
Explain FAST_SCAN vs SCAN compilation modes
Document Eigen library usage for matrix operations
Include annotated code snippets with explanations
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Analyze GLM5 KV Cache Architecture
userStories.description: Study the local GLM5 repository to understand its KV cache structure.

READ the actual code in /home/lily/wsl_git/glm5 - do NOT just web search
Document the directory structure and key files
Find and document KV cache tensor shapes: [batch, heads, seq_len, head_dim]
Identify the attention implementation file(s) with exact paths
Document how K and V are stored, accessed, and evicted
Identify any existing quantization or compression code

userStories.title: Implement Random Orthogonal Matrix Generation
userStories.description: Generate random orthogonal transformation matrices for RaBitQ.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/rotation.hpp and src/rotation.cpp
Generate random matrix with i.i.d. N(0,1) entries
Implement QR decomposition using Eigen's HouseholderQR
Extract orthogonal Q matrix from QR decomposition
Unit tests verify: P·P^T = I (within numerical tolerance 1e-6)
Unit tests verify: det(P) = ±1 (orthogonal)
Benchmark matrix generation time for D = 128, 256, 512, 960

userStories.title: Implement RaBitQ Data Quantization (Index Phase)

userStories.title: Implement RaBitQ Query Processing

Implement inner product via AND + popcount

Unit tests verify unbiasedness: average error ≈ 0 over 10000 samples
